name: Natural Minor
interval_formula: W H W W H W W
feel: Dark, emotive, melancholic — the backbone of UK garage
analytical_note: The flatted 3rd, 6th, and 7th give the dark quality vs major. In E minor: E F# G A B C D. Most garage tracks live here — it's the default emotional register.

name: Dorian Mode
interval_formula: W H W W W H W
feel: Minor but with a lift — jazzy, soulful. Slightly warmer than natural minor.
analytical_note: Same as natural minor but the 6th is 1 semitone higher. In E dorian: E F# G A B C# D. That raised 6th adds brightness. The iv chord becomes a minor (not diminished), which sounds warmer.

name: Phrygian Mode
interval_formula: H W W W H W W
feel: Tense, dark, Arabic flavour. The b2 creates a half-step pull back to root.
examples: MPH — Raw (E phrygian bass line: E→F)
analytical_note: The b2 (just one semitone above root) creates instant tension. In E phrygian: E F G A B C D. The MPH bass moves E→F — that F is the b2 (phrygian note). One semitone up, immediately pulls back down. That's the signature tension.

name: Harmonic Minor
interval_formula: W H W W H Aug2 H
feel: Dark with a dramatic pull — the raised 7th creates a strong resolution back to root. Exotic flavour from the augmented 2nd gap.
examples: Sammy Virji — Cops & Robbers (G# minor, V major chord = harmonic minor influence)
analytical_note: Raise the 7th by 1 semitone vs natural minor. This makes the V chord major (not minor), which creates a strong dominant resolution. Sammy Virji's G#maj stab (instead of G#m) comes from this — the B# in the G# major chord is the raised 7th of G# harmonic minor.
